headlines.title: Ebola death toll rises as two Americans are flown to Europe for treatment
headlines.description: The suspected death toll from the Ebola outbreak in central Africa rose again Wednesday, as a group of potentially exposed Americans were arriving in Europe for monitoring and the World Health Organization warned a vaccine was still months away

headlines.title: American doctor with Ebola flown to Germany after operating on patient with deadly virus
headlines.description: By the time the American surgeon who contracted Ebola was flown to Germany, he was barely able to stand on his own, according to leaders of the Christian missionary group where he worked.

headlines.title: From Omaha Steaks to hazmat suits: What life was like for contagion patients in quarantine
headlines.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the country’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa
headlines.description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Americans exposed to hantavirus forced to quarantine
headlines.description: The CDC is now saying that the Americans quarantining at a Nebraska facility after being exposed to Hantavirus are not allowed to leave, after initially saying the quarantine was voluntary. This, as the WHO monitors hundreds of suspected Ebola cases in an outbreak in Africa. NBC News’ Maggie Vespa has the latest.

headlines.title: How to Navigate a Break-Up With a Friend
headlines.description: Sometimes a friendship breakup can sting more than a romantic one, so women’s relational health educator Danielle Bayard-Jackson joins TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones to talk about how to navigate a platonic split. She explains how to know when a friendship is worth fighting for by asking yourself simple questions, shares helpful dialogue to use when having conversations, and highlights the importance of being clear and direct with what you want.

headlines.title: How Women Can Prioritize Health — and What to Check Annually
headlines.description: More than 6 in 10 women struggle prioritizing their health according to a survey, so Dr. Elisa Port, chief breast cancer surgeon at Mount Sinai and author of “Breast Advice,” joins TODAY with a closer look at the biggest issues and questions women face regarding their health. She breaks down the four Ws that help reduce risk of breast cancer, shares her recommendations for prioritizing screenings, and highlights the benefits and risks of hormone replacement therapy.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.

headlines.title: I’ve tested dozens of workout headphones: These are the best
headlines.description: I tested over a dozen workout headphones from brands like Apple, Beats, and Shokz to find our top picks for running, biking, the gym and more.
